rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

[Table Level]
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. metadata.title_summary:
title: Introduction 
summary: LCAA, a blank check company established on January 5, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, aims to execute a business combination through mergers or asset acquisitions. After exploring various opportunities, it entered into an Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, which was later amended on October 11, 2023. The company plans to finance the business combination using funds from its IPO, the sale of LCAA Private Warrants, and potential PIPE financing. Currently classified as a "shell company" under the Exchange Act, LCAA has no operations and primarily holds cash assets.

Co-led by Mr. Chinta Bhagat and Mr. Scott Chen, both Co-CEOs and Managing Partners of L Catterton Asia, LCAA is backed by a prominent global investment firm specializing in consumer-focused investments, managing around $34 billion in equity capital. The LCA Acquisition Sponsor, LP, is controlled by L Catterton Asia 3, which focuses on consumer investments in Asia.

LCAA completed its IPO on March 15, 2021, raising $286,508,741, which is held in a trust account for investment in U.S. government securities. Following an Extension Extraordinary General Meeting on March 10, 2023, shareholders approved an amendment to extend the deadline for completing a business combination to June 15, 2023, with the possibility of further extensions. By June 2023, the Board established an Extension Committee that approved multiple one-month extensions, pushing the deadline to February 15, 2024.

title: Eletre 
summary: The Eletre represents Lotus's first foray into the electric vehicle market as a pure electric SUV and lifestyle vehicle, measuring 5,103 mm in length with a 3,019 mm wheelbase, prioritizing spaciousness and comfort. It features five distinct drive modes that can be adjusted through various vehicle systems, emphasizing aerodynamics with a design that enhances airflow to minimize resistance. The interior is equipped with durable materials and a 15.1-inch OLED screen, alongside a digital passenger display and an augmented reality (AR) head-up display (HUD). The vehicle operates on Lotus Hyper OS, which provides advanced digital mapping and connectivity, including 5G compatibility for over-the-air updates. Safety features include collision mitigation, traffic alerts, and lane departure warnings.

Customers have the option to cancel their orders; however, deposits are generally non-refundable under specific conditions. The Eletre is projected to achieve an annual sales volume of 40,000 to 50,000 units starting in 2026.

In addition to the Eletre, Lotus is set to launch the Emeya (Type 133), its second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer, built on the same architecture as the Eletre. The Emeya boasts impressive performance, accelerating from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.8 seconds. It features supercharging capabilities, allowing for a range of 150 km in five minutes and achieving 80% capacity in 18 minutes with a 350 kW charger. Deliveries of the Emeya commenced in China in March 2024, with European deliveries anticipated in the second half of 2024. The average Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the Emeya exceeds $100,000, with expected annual sales of 30,000 to 40,000 units starting in 2028.

Overall, both the Eletre and Emeya highlight Lotus's commitment to innovation in the electric vehicle segment, combining advanced technology, performance, and luxury in their designs.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Introduction 
summary: LCAA is a blank check company established on January 5, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, with the goal of executing a business combination through mergers or asset acquisitions. After evaluating various opportunities, LCAA entered into an Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, which was later amended on October 11, 2023. The company plans to finance this business combination using cash from its IPO, proceeds from the sale of LCAA Private Warrants, and potential PIPE financing. As a "shell company" under the Exchange Act, LCAA currently has no operations and minimal assets, primarily cash.

The leadership team includes Co-CEOs Mr. Chinta Bhagat and Mr. Scott Chen, both associated with L Catterton Asia, a significant global investment firm managing around $34 billion in equity capital. LCAA completed its IPO on March 15, 2021, raising $286.5 million through the sale of 25 million units and private warrants, with proceeds held in a trust account for specific investments.

On March 10, 2023, shareholders approved an extension of the deadline for completing a business combination from March 15, 2023, to June 15, 2023, with the possibility of further extensions granted to the Board. Following a series of shareholder redemptions, approximately $222.7 million remains in the trust account, with 21.8 million public shares outstanding. The Board has since extended the deadline to February 15, 2024, with provisions for additional one-month extensions, supported by financial commitments from the Sponsor. The trust account funds will only be released under certain conditions related to the completion of the business combination or specific shareholder actions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA are the result of extensive negotiations following LCAA's establishment as a blank check company in January 2021 by affiliates of L Catterton. LCAA's primary goal is to facilitate business combinations, and it raised $294.7 million through its IPO in March 2021. Although LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, including seven specific companies, it ultimately focused on LTC for a proposed merger. 

The discussions between LCAA and LTC began in early 2022, with LCAA representatives conducting thorough due diligence on LTC's business and financial performance. Throughout the negotiation process, valuation discussions fluctuated due to market conditions, with LTC's enterprise value initially proposed at $12 billion but later adjusted to a range of $5.5 billion to $10 billion. 

On January 31, 2023, the parties finalized key agreements, including the Original Merger Agreement, which established the terms for the business combination. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made, including provisions for PIPE financing and adjustments to the Sponsor Support Agreement. The merger aims to enhance strategic growth and operational efficiencies for both entities, with a focus on leveraging synergies to create shareholder value.

As of October 2023, the parties signed an amended agreement that revised the structure of the merger, ensuring that LCAA Public Shareholders would receive LTC American Depositary Shares instead of Ordinary Shares. Additionally, the Sponsor Support Agreement was amended to adjust the terms regarding the forfeiture and vesting of Sponsor Shares, reflecting the evolving nature of the partnership. Overall, the merger is positioned to capitalize on market opportunities and strengthen the combined entity's competitive positioning.

title: Our Vehicles 
summary: Lotus, a prestigious British luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer with over 70 years of expertise in high-performance automotive design, is redefining the electric vehicle market with its innovative lifestyle offerings. The company's first production vehicle, the Eletre, is a state-of-the-art all-electric SUV that showcases advanced technology and performance, featuring a proprietary 800-volt EPA powertrain. Launched in 2023, the Eletre combines a sleek aerodynamic design with a luxurious interior and cutting-edge connectivity features, including a 15.1-inch OLED display and 5G compatibility for real-time updates. It offers three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to varying customer preferences, with impressive acceleration and range capabilities.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced the Emeya, a four-door luxury hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) that emphasizes sustainable performance and advanced technology. Set to begin deliveries in March 2024, the Emeya features rapid acceleration and supercharging capabilities, appealing to eco-conscious drivers who prioritize both luxury and performance. With plans for additional electric models, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026, Lotus is committed to continuous innovation and sustainability in the automotive industry.

The brand's future models will focus on adapting to evolving societal needs, emphasizing sustainability, digital transformation, and user-centric design. By embracing collaboration and change, Lotus aims to remain competitive and relevant in an ever-evolving market, setting benchmarks for future electric vehicles while maintaining its heritage of excellence in engineering and design.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Q:  Why is LCAA proposing the Business Combination? 
summary: LCAA was formed to enable various business combinations, such as mergers and acquisitions. It went public on March 15, 2021, raising $250 million through the sale of 25 million Units, each consisting of one Class A Ordinary Share and one-third of a Public Warrant. A private placement of 5 million Private Warrants added another $7.5 million, and subsequent underwriter activity brought total gross proceeds to approximately $294.7 million, with most funds allocated to a trust account for public shareholders. After incurring around $5.7 million in underwriting fees, LCAA sought to extend its business combination deadline from March 15, 2023, to June 15, 2023, which was approved by shareholders on March 10, 2023. This extension allows the Board to further prolong the deadline up to nine times without additional shareholder votes, potentially extending it to March 15, 2024. Following the extension vote, 23.97% of public shares were redeemed, withdrawing about $70.2 million from the trust account, leaving roughly $222.7 million and 21.8 million shares outstanding. On June 5, 2023, the Board approved a further extension to July 15, 2023, with an established Extension Committee to manage subsequent extensions. The committee has since approved seven additional one-month extensions, with the latest extending the deadline to February 15, 2024. LCAA is optimistic about a potential business combination with LTC, viewing it as a company with significant growth prospects and a strong market position.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational Benchmarking 
summary: The LCAA Board conducted a comprehensive analysis of Lotus Tech's financial performance, focusing on key metrics such as estimated revenue growth rate, gross margin, and EBITDA margin in comparison to its peers, including Tesla, Lucid, Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin. This evaluation utilized Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts for the years 2023 to 2025, based on publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.

The analysis revealed that Lotus Tech is projected to achieve a remarkable revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 90.0%. However, it faces challenges with gross margins estimated between -18.0% and -23.0%, while its EBITDA margin for 2025 is expected to exceed 5.0%. In contrast, Tesla is anticipated to have a revenue CAGR of 28.4% with improving gross margins and a robust EBITDA margin of 21% in 2025. Lucid is expected to lead with the highest revenue CAGR at 125.7%, but it will struggle with negative gross margins initially. Other competitors like Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin show varying growth rates and profitability metrics, with Ferrari and Porsche exhibiting strong gross margins and EBITDA margins.

Additionally, the Board reviewed the estimated enterprise value-to-sales (EV/Sales) multiples for Lotus Tech against its peers for 2024 and 2025. Lotus Tech's EV/Sales multiple is projected to be approximately 0.9x in 2024, declining to around 0.6x in 2025. In comparison, Tesla's multiples are significantly higher, starting at 7.2x in 2024 and decreasing to 5.6x in 2025. Ferrari leads the group with the highest multiples, estimated at 8.4x for 2024 and 7.7x for 2025, while other brands like Polestar, NIO, and Aston Martin exhibit lower multiples.

Overall, the analysis provides a detailed overview of Lotus Tech's ambitious growth trajectory alongside its established competitors, highlighting both the challenges and opportunities within the luxury sports car market. The findings are summarized in accompanying tables that outline operational metrics and enterprise value-to-sales multiples, reflecting market perceptions of growth and profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: WESTWOOD CAPITAL GROUP COMMITTED EQUITY FINANCING
summary: On September 16, 2024, a Purchase Agreement and Registration Rights Agreement were established between our company and Westwood Capital Group, enabling Westwood to purchase up to US$350 million of American Depositary Shares (ADSs) at our discretion. This agreement is contingent upon the conditions outlined within it and has a commitment period of approximately 36 months, commencing from the ELOC Closing Date, which begins once the registration statement for the resale of ADSs is declared effective by the SEC.

Under this agreement, while we have the ability to sell ADSs to Westwood, there is no obligation for Westwood to purchase any specific amount. The actual sales will be influenced by various factors, including market conditions and our funding requirements. Currently, a total of 44,450,000 ADSs are registered for resale, which includes 700,000 Commitment ADSs issued to Westwood as compensation for its commitment, along with up to 43,750,000 ADSs that may be sold thereafter. Should the total number of ADSs sold exceed this registered amount, additional registration under the Securities Act will be necessary, which could lead to significant dilution for existing shareholders.

The Purchase Agreement also imposes restrictions on directing Westwood to purchase ADSs if such purchases would exceed a specified Beneficial Ownership Limitation. The net proceeds from these sales will vary based on the frequency and pricing of the ADSs sold, and are intended to fund product innovation, the development of automobility technologies, the expansion of our global distribution network, and general corporate purposes. Additionally, Commitment Ordinary Shares were issued to Westwood on September 19, 2024, as part of this irrevocable commitment.
